evidence.title: README (segment 2)
evidence.excerpt: Generate visualizations of GitHub user and repository statistics with GitHub Actions. Visualizations can include data for both private repositories, and for repositories you have contributed to, but do not own.

evidence.title: README (segment 3)
evidence.excerpt: Generated images automatically switch between GitHub light theme and GitHub dark theme.

evidence.title: README (segment 4)
evidence.excerpt: When someone views a profile on GitHub, it is often because they are curious about a user's open source projects and contributions. Unfortunately, that user's stars, forks, and pinned repositories do not necessarily reflect the contributions they make to private repositories. The data likewise does not present a complete picture of the user's total contributions beyond the current year.

evidence.title: README (segment 5)
evidence.excerpt: This project aims to collect a variety of profile and repository statistics using the GitHub API. It then generates images that can be displayed in repository READMEs, or in a user's [Profile README](https://docs.github.com/en/github/setting-up-and-managing-your-github-profile/managing-your-profile-readme).

evidence.title: README (segment 6)
evidence.excerpt: Since the project runs on GitHub Actions, no server is required to regularly regenerate the images with updated statistics. Likewise, since the user runs the analysis code themselves via GitHub Actions, they can use their GitHub access token to collect statistics on private repositories that an external service would be unable to access.

evidence.title: README (segment 7)
evidence.excerpt: If the project is used with an access token that has sufficient permissions to read private repositories, it may leak details about those repositories in error messages. For example, the `aiohttp` library—used for asynchronous API requests—may include the requested URL in exceptions, which can leak the name of private repositories. If there is an exception caused by `aiohttp`, this exception will be viewable in the Actions tab of the repository fork, and anyone may be able to see the name of one or more private repositories.

evidence.title: README (segment 8)
evidence.excerpt: Due to some issues with the GitHub statistics API, there are some situations where it returns inaccurate results. Specifically, the repository view count statistics and total lines of code modified are probably somewhat inaccurate. Unexpectedly, these values will become more accurate over time as GitHub caches statistics for your repositories. Additionally, repositories that were last contributed to more than a year ago may not be included in the statistics due to limitations in the results returned by the API.

evidence.title: Package as an action/separate codebase from generated images
evidence.excerpt: Package as an action/separate codebase from generated images (state:open)

evidence.title: ROOT_FILES
evidence.excerpt: .gitattributes, .github, .gitignore, LICENSE, README.md, generate_images.py, generated, github_stats.py, requirements.txt, templates
